```{r}
# Shares-only extracts (to avoid duplicate cols in joins)
df_16_share <- df_16_full %>% select(county_fips, rep_share_16 = rep_share)
df_20_share <- df_20_full %>% select(county_fips, rep_share_20 = rep_share)
df_24_share <- df_24_full %>% select(county_fips, rep_share_24 = rep_share)


# ==========================================
# 2. TRAIN: 2016 -> 2020 shift, predictors = 2016 embeddings
# ==========================================

train_shift <- inner_join(df_16_share, df_20_share, by = "county_fips") %>%
  # attach 2016 embeddings + metadata we want to keep
  inner_join(
    df_16_full %>%
      select(county_fips, county_name, state_po, vote_density, ends_with("_mean")),
    by = "county_fips"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    SHIFT_16_20 = rep_share_20 - rep_share_16
  ) %>%
  select(
    county_fips,
    county_name,
    state_po,
    vote_density,
    rep_share_16,
    rep_share_20,      # kept for analysis, not used for training
    SHIFT_16_20,       # target for training
    ends_with("_mean") # predictors = 2016 embeddings
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

cat("TRAIN data loaded (2016->2020) for", nrow(train_shift), "counties.\n")
cat("Avg TRAIN shift:", round(mean(train_shift$SHIFT_16_20) * 100, 2), "points\n")


# ==========================================
# 3. TEST: 2020 -> 2024 shift, predictors = 2020 embeddings
# ==========================================

test_shift <- inner_join(df_20_share, df_24_share, by = "county_fips") %>%
  # attach 2020 embeddings + metadata we want to keep
  inner_join(
    df_20_full %>%
      select(county_fips, county_name, state_po, vote_density, ends_with("_mean")),
    by = "county_fips"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    SHIFT_20_24 = rep_share_24 - rep_share_20
  ) %>%
  select(
    county_fips,
    county_name,
    state_po,
    vote_density,
    rep_share_20,      # kept for analysis, not used for training
    SHIFT_20_24,       # target only for evaluation
    ends_with("_mean") # predictors = 2020 embeddings
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

cat("\nTEST data loaded (2020->2024) for", nrow(test_shift), "counties.\n")
cat("Avg TEST shift:", round(mean(test_shift$SHIFT_20_24) * 100, 2), "points\n")


# ==========================================
# OPTIONAL: Remove extreme shift outliers (|SHIFT| > 0.15)
#   Drop this RIGHT AFTER you create train_shift and test_shift (after na.omit()).
#   Easy to comment out as a block.
# ==========================================

outlier_cutoff <- 0.15

# --- TRAIN outliers (2016 -> 2020) ---
n_train_before <- nrow(train_shift)
train_shift <- train_shift %>% filter(abs(SHIFT_16_20) <= outlier_cutoff)
n_train_after <- nrow(train_shift)

cat("\n[Outlier filter | TRAIN] Before:", n_train_before,
    "| After:", n_train_after,
    "| Removed:", (n_train_before - n_train_after), "\n")
cat("[Outlier filter | TRAIN] Shift range now:",
    round(min(train_shift$SHIFT_16_20), 4), "to",
    round(max(train_shift$SHIFT_16_20), 4), "\n")

# --- TEST outliers (2020 -> 2024) ---
n_test_before <- nrow(test_shift)
test_shift <- test_shift %>% filter(abs(SHIFT_20_24) <= outlier_cutoff)
n_test_after <- nrow(test_shift)

cat("\n[Outlier filter | TEST]  Before:", n_test_before,
    "| After:", n_test_after,
    "| Removed:", (n_test_before - n_test_after), "\n")
cat("[Outlier filter | TEST]  Shift range now:",
    round(min(test_shift$SHIFT_20_24), 4), "to",
    round(max(test_shift$SHIFT_20_24), 4), "\n")


# ==========================================
# 4. Make sure TRAIN and TEST have identical predictor columns
# ==========================================

id_cols_train <- c("county_fips", "county_name", "state_po", "vote_density", "rep_share_16", "rep_share_20", "SHIFT_16_20")
id_cols_test  <- c("county_fips", "county_name", "state_po", "vote_density", "rep_share_20", "SHIFT_20_24")

x_cols_train <- setdiff(names(train_shift), id_cols_train)
x_cols_test  <- setdiff(names(test_shift),  id_cols_test)

common_x <- intersect(x_cols_train, x_cols_test)

train_shift <- train_shift %>% select(all_of(id_cols_train), all_of(common_x))
test_shift  <- test_shift  %>% select(all_of(id_cols_test),  all_of(common_x))

cat("\nUsing", length(common_x), "shared embedding predictors.\n")


# ==========================================
# 5. 5-Fold CV ON TRAIN (2016->2020), predict TEST (2020->2024)
# ==========================================

set.seed(55)
train_shift <- train_shift %>%
  mutate(fold_id = sample(rep(1:5, length.out = n())))

test_preds_by_fold <- list()
importances <- list()

cat("\nStarting CV: train on 2016->2020 (2016 embeddings), predict 2020->2024 (2020 embeddings)...\n")

for (k in 1:5) {

  train_df <- train_shift %>% filter(fold_id != k)

  rf_model <- ranger(
    SHIFT_16_20 ~ . - county_fips - county_name - state_po - vote_density - rep_share_16 - rep_share_20 - fold_id,
    data       = train_df,
    num.trees  = 500,
    mtry       = 21,
    importance = "impurity",
    seed       = 42
  )

  preds <- predict(rf_model, data = test_shift)$predictions

  tmp <- test_shift %>%
    mutate(
      fold_model = k,
      Predicted_Shift = preds
    )

  test_preds_by_fold[[k]] <- tmp
  importances[[k]] <- rf_model$variable.importance

  cat("  Fold", k, "model trained (2016->2020) and applied to 2020->2024.\n")
}

results <- bind_rows(test_preds_by_fold) %>%
  group_by(county_fips, county_name, state_po, vote_density, rep_share_20, SHIFT_20_24) %>%
  summarise(
    Predicted_Shift = mean(Predicted_Shift),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Error = Predicted_Shift - SHIFT_20_24,
    Abs_Error = abs(Error)
  )


# ==========================================
# 6. Metrics (evaluated on 2020->2024)
# ==========================================

rss  <- sum(results$Error^2)
tss  <- sum((results$SHIFT_20_24 - mean(results$SHIFT_20_24))^2)
r2   <- 1 - (rss / tss)
rmse <- sqrt(mean(results$Error^2))

cat("\n==========================================")
cat("\nTRANSFER MODEL RESULTS (EARLY-YEAR EMBEDDINGS)")
cat("\nTrain: 2016->2020 (2016 emb) | Test: 2020->2024 (2020 emb)")
cat("\n==========================================")
cat("\nR-Squared: ", round(r2, 3))
cat("\nRMSE:      ", round(rmse, 4), "(", round(rmse*100, 2), "percentage points )")
cat("\n==========================================\n")


# ==========================================
# 7. Visualizations (same as before)
# ==========================================

p1 <- ggplot(results, aes(x = SHIFT_20_24, y = Predicted_Shift)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1, color = "purple") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "orange", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Early-year embeddings: 2016→2020 trained, 2020→2024 predicted",
    subtitle = paste0("R² = ", round(r2, 3), " | RMSE = ", round(rmse, 4)),
    x = "Actual Shift (2020 → 2024)",
    y = "Predicted Shift (trained on 2016 → 2020)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_minimal()
print(p1)

p2 <- ggplot(results, aes(x = rep_share_20, y = Error)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "blue", fill = "gray90") +
  labs(
    title = "Where were the surprises (transfer model, early embeddings)?",
    subtitle = "Positive Error = model expected a more pro-GOP shift than happened",
    x = "2020 GOP Vote Share (Baseline)",
    y = "Prediction Error (Pred - Actual)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_minimal()
print(p2)

avg_imp <- bind_rows(lapply(importances, function(x) as.data.frame(t(x)))) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), mean)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Feature", values_to = "Importance") %>%
  arrange(desc(Importance)) %>%
  slice(1:15)

p3 <- ggplot(avg_imp, aes(x = reorder(Feature, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkcyan") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Top features (learned from 2016 embeddings)",
    subtitle = "Top 15 features by impurity importance (avg across folds)",
    x = "",
    y = "Variable Importance (Impurity)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
print(p3)

# ==========================================
# 9. Visualization 4: R² by Vote Density Decile (TRANSFER MODEL)
#   Drop this right after p3 (and before write_csv).
# ==========================================

# Helper to compute R² safely (returns NA if variance is zero)
r2_fun <- function(actual, pred) {
  rss <- sum((pred - actual)^2, na.rm = TRUE)
  tss <- sum((actual - mean(actual, na.rm = TRUE))^2, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (is.na(tss) || tss == 0) return(NA_real_)
  1 - rss / tss
}

r2_by_density_decile <- results %>%
  mutate(
    density_decile = ntile(vote_density, 10)
    # If density is super skewed, you may prefer:
    # density_decile = ntile(log1p(vote_density), 10)
  ) %>%
  group_by(density_decile) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    r2 = r2_fun(SHIFT_20_24, Predicted_Shift),
    rmse = sqrt(mean((Predicted_Shift - SHIFT_20_24)^2, na.rm = TRUE)),
    density_min = min(vote_density, na.rm = TRUE),
    density_max = max(vote_density, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

print(r2_by_density_decile)

p4 <- ggplot(r2_by_density_decile, aes(x = factor(density_decile), y = r2)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Transfer-model performance by vote density",
    subtitle = "R² computed within each vote_density decile (test period: 2020→2024)",
    x = "Vote Density Decile (1 = lowest density, 10 = highest)",
    y = "R² (within decile)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p4)

# ==========================================
# 8. Save
# ==========================================

write_csv(results, "data/results/county_change_m2_early_embeddings_results.csv")
cat("Saved: data/results/county_change_m2_early_embeddings_results.csv\n")
```

```{r}

## CHANGE to voter turnout outliers

library(tidyverse)
library(ranger)

# ==============================================================================
# GOAL (DELTA MODEL):
#   Train on 2016 -> 2020 shifts using DELTA embeddings (2020 - 2016),
#   then predict 2020 -> 2024 shifts using DELTA embeddings (2024 - 2020),
#   WITHOUT training on 2020 -> 2024 shifts.
#
#   Predictors: Axx_mean deltas only (suffix: _delta)
#   Target (train): SHIFT_16_20
#   Eval (test):    SHIFT_20_24
# ==============================================================================

# ==========================================
# 0. Load base files
# ==========================================

df_16 <- read_csv("data/prep/counties/clean_county_emb_16_17.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
df_20 <- read_csv("data/prep/counties/clean_county_emb_20.csv",     show_col_types = FALSE)
df_24 <- read_csv("data/prep/counties/clean_county_emb_24.csv",     show_col_types = FALSE)

# We'll use only the _mean embeddings for deltas (consistent with your earlier scripts)
emb_cols_16 <- names(df_16) %>% keep(~ str_detect(.x, "_mean$"))
emb_cols_20 <- names(df_20) %>% keep(~ str_detect(.x, "_mean$"))
emb_cols_24 <- names(df_24) %>% keep(~ str_detect(.x, "_mean$"))

common_emb <- Reduce(intersect, list(emb_cols_16, emb_cols_20, emb_cols_24))

cat("Using", length(common_emb), "shared *_mean embedding columns for deltas.\n")

# ==========================================
# 1. TRAIN data: 2016 vs 2020 (delta = 2020 - 2016)
# ==========================================

df_16_small <- df_16 %>%
  select(
    county_fips,
    rep_share_16 = rep_share,
    all_of(common_emb)
  ) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_16"), all_of(common_emb))

df_20_small <- df_20 %>%
  select(
    county_fips,
    county_name,
    state_po,
    vote_density,
    rep_share_20 = rep_share,
    all_of(common_emb)
  ) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_20"), all_of(common_emb))

train_join <- inner_join(df_16_small, df_20_small, by = "county_fips") %>%
  mutate(SHIFT_16_20 = rep_share_20 - rep_share_16)

# Build delta predictors safely (NO cur_column)
train_deltas <- purrr::map_dfc(common_emb, function(nm) {
  train_join[[paste0(nm, "_20")]] - train_join[[paste0(nm, "_16")]]
})
names(train_deltas) <- paste0(common_emb, "_delta")

train_shift <- bind_cols(train_join, train_deltas) %>%
  select(
    county_fips, county_name, state_po, vote_density,
    rep_share_16, rep_share_20,
    SHIFT_16_20,
    ends_with("_delta")
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

cat("\nTRAIN data loaded (2016->2020) for", nrow(train_shift), "counties.\n")
cat("Avg TRAIN shift:", round(mean(train_shift$SHIFT_16_20) * 100, 2), "points\n")

# ==========================================
# 2. TEST data: 2020 vs 2024 (delta = 2024 - 2020)
# ==========================================

df_20_base <- df_20 %>%
  select(
    county_fips,
    rep_share_20 = rep_share,
    all_of(common_emb)
  ) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_20"), all_of(common_emb))

df_24_small <- df_24 %>%
  select(
    county_fips,
    county_name,
    state_po,
    vote_density,
    rep_share_24 = rep_share,
    all_of(common_emb)
  ) %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_24"), all_of(common_emb))

test_join <- inner_join(df_20_base, df_24_small, by = "county_fips") %>%
  mutate(SHIFT_20_24 = rep_share_24 - rep_share_20)

# Build delta predictors safely (NO cur_column)
test_deltas <- purrr::map_dfc(common_emb, function(nm) {
  test_join[[paste0(nm, "_24")]] - test_join[[paste0(nm, "_20")]]
})
names(test_deltas) <- paste0(common_emb, "_delta")

test_shift <- bind_cols(test_join, test_deltas) %>%
  select(
    county_fips, county_name, state_po, vote_density,
    rep_share_20,
    SHIFT_20_24,
    ends_with("_delta")
  ) %>%
  na.omit()

cat("\nTEST data loaded (2020->2024) for", nrow(test_shift), "counties.\n")
cat("Avg TEST shift:", round(mean(test_shift$SHIFT_20_24) * 100, 2), "points\n")

# ==========================================
# OPTIONAL: Remove extreme shift outliers (|SHIFT| > 0.15)
#   Drop this RIGHT AFTER you create train_shift and test_shift (after na.omit()).
#   Easy to comment out as a block.
# ==========================================

outlier_cutoff <- 0.15

# --- TRAIN outliers (2016 -> 2020) ---
n_train_before <- nrow(train_shift)
train_shift <- train_shift %>% filter(abs(SHIFT_16_20) <= outlier_cutoff)
n_train_after <- nrow(train_shift)

cat("\n[Outlier filter | TRAIN] Before:", n_train_before,
    "| After:", n_train_after,
    "| Removed:", (n_train_before - n_train_after), "\n")
cat("[Outlier filter | TRAIN] Shift range now:",
    round(min(train_shift$SHIFT_16_20), 4), "to",
    round(max(train_shift$SHIFT_16_20), 4), "\n")

# --- TEST outliers (2020 -> 2024) ---
n_test_before <- nrow(test_shift)
test_shift <- test_shift %>% filter(abs(SHIFT_20_24) <= outlier_cutoff)
n_test_after <- nrow(test_shift)

cat("\n[Outlier filter | TEST]  Before:", n_test_before,
    "| After:", n_test_after,
    "| Removed:", (n_test_before - n_test_after), "\n")
cat("[Outlier filter | TEST]  Shift range now:",
    round(min(test_shift$SHIFT_20_24), 4), "to",
    round(max(test_shift$SHIFT_20_24), 4), "\n")

# ==========================================
# 3. Ensure identical predictor columns between TRAIN and TEST
# ==========================================

id_cols_train <- c("county_fips", "county_name", "state_po", "vote_density",
                   "rep_share_16", "rep_share_20", "SHIFT_16_20")
id_cols_test  <- c("county_fips", "county_name", "state_po", "vote_density",
                   "rep_share_20", "SHIFT_20_24")

x_cols_train <- setdiff(names(train_shift), id_cols_train)
x_cols_test  <- setdiff(names(test_shift),  id_cols_test)

common_x <- intersect(x_cols_train, x_cols_test)

train_shift <- train_shift %>% select(all_of(id_cols_train), all_of(common_x))
test_shift  <- test_shift  %>% select(all_of(id_cols_test),  all_of(common_x))

cat("\nUsing", length(common_x), "shared DELTA predictors.\n")

# ==========================================
# 4. 5-Fold CV on TRAIN (2016->2020), predict TEST (2020->2024)
# ==========================================

set.seed(55)
train_shift <- train_shift %>%
  mutate(fold_id = sample(rep(1:5, length.out = n())))

test_preds_by_fold <- list()
importances <- list()

cat("\nStarting CV: train on 2016->2020 DELTAS, predict 2020->2024 DELTAS...\n")

for (k in 1:5) {

  train_df <- train_shift %>% filter(fold_id != k)

  rf_model <- ranger(
    SHIFT_16_20 ~ . - county_fips - county_name - state_po - vote_density - rep_share_16 - rep_share_20 - fold_id,
    data       = train_df,
    num.trees  = 500,
    mtry       = min(21, length(common_x)),  # guard if fewer predictors
    importance = "impurity",
    seed       = 42
  )

  preds <- predict(rf_model, data = test_shift)$predictions

  tmp <- test_shift %>%
    mutate(
      fold_model = k,
      Predicted_Shift = preds
    )

  test_preds_by_fold[[k]] <- tmp
  importances[[k]] <- rf_model$variable.importance

  cat("  Fold", k, "model trained (on 2016->2020) and applied to 2020->2024.\n")
}

# Average predictions across the 5 independently-trained models
results <- bind_rows(test_preds_by_fold) %>%
  group_by(county_fips, county_name, state_po, vote_density, rep_share_20, SHIFT_20_24) %>%
  summarise(
    Predicted_Shift = mean(Predicted_Shift),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Error = Predicted_Shift - SHIFT_20_24,
    Abs_Error = abs(Error)
  )

# ==========================================
# 5. Metrics (evaluated on 2020->2024)
# ==========================================

rss  <- sum(results$Error^2)
tss  <- sum((results$SHIFT_20_24 - mean(results$SHIFT_20_24))^2)
r2   <- 1 - (rss / tss)
rmse <- sqrt(mean(results$Error^2))

cat("\n==========================================")
cat("\nDELTA TRANSFER MODEL RESULTS")
cat("\nTrain: 2016->2020 (delta 20-16) | Test: 2020->2024 (delta 24-20)")
cat("\n==========================================")
cat("\nR-Squared: ", round(r2, 3))
cat("\nRMSE:      ", round(rmse, 4), "(", round(rmse*100, 2), "percentage points )")
cat("\n==========================================\n")

# ==========================================
# 6. Visualization 1: Actual vs Predicted Shift
# ==========================================

p1 <- ggplot(results, aes(x = SHIFT_20_24, y = Predicted_Shift)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 1, color = "purple") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "orange", se = FALSE) +
  labs(
    title = "Delta-embeddings transfer: (20-16) trained, (24-20) predicted",
    subtitle = paste0("R² = ", round(r2, 3), " | RMSE = ", round(rmse, 4)),
    x = "Actual Shift (2020 → 2024)",
    y = "Predicted Shift (trained on 2016 → 2020 deltas)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p1)

# ==========================================
# 7. Visualization 2: Error vs Baseline Partisanship
# ==========================================

p2 <- ggplot(results, aes(x = rep_share_20, y = Error)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "blue", fill = "gray90") +
  labs(
    title = "Where were the surprises (delta transfer model)?",
    subtitle = "Positive Error = model expected a more pro-GOP shift than happened",
    x = "2020 GOP Vote Share (Baseline)",
    y = "Prediction Error (Pred - Actual)"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p2)

# ==========================================
# 8. Visualization 3: Top Predictive Delta Features
# ==========================================

avg_imp <- bind_rows(lapply(importances, function(x) as.data.frame(t(x)))) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), mean)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "Feature", values_to = "Importance") %>%
  arrange(desc(Importance)) %>%
  slice(1:15)

p3 <- ggplot(avg_imp, aes(x = reorder(Feature, Importance), y = Importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "darkcyan") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Which delta embeddings predict swings (learned from 2016→2020)?",
    subtitle = "Top 15 delta features by impurity importance (avg across folds)",
    x = "",
    y = "Variable Importance (Impurity)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p3)

# ==========================================
# 9. OPTIONAL DROP-IN VIS: R² by vote_density decile
#   - Put this AFTER you create `results` and compute r2/rmse (anywhere after results exists).
# ==========================================

results_r2_by_decile <- results %>%
  mutate(
    density_decile = ntile(vote_density, 10)
  ) %>%
  group_by(density_decile) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    rss = sum(Error^2),
    tss = sum((SHIFT_20_24 - mean(SHIFT_20_24))^2),
    r2  = 1 - rss / tss,
    rmse = sqrt(mean(Error^2)),
    density_min = min(vote_density, na.rm = TRUE),
    density_max = max(vote_density, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

p4 <- ggplot(results_r2_by_decile, aes(x = factor(density_decile), y = r2)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Model performance by vote-density decile (Delta transfer model)",
    subtitle = "R² computed within each decile on the 2020→2024 test set",
    x = "Vote-density decile (1 = lowest density, 10 = highest)",
    y = "R² within decile"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(p4)

# ==========================================
# 10. Save
# ==========================================

write_csv(results, "data/results/county_change_m3_delta_results.csv")
cat("Saved: data/results/county_change_m3_delta_results.csv\n")

```

# Let's validate the data!

```{r}

library(tidyverse)

df_16 <- read.csv("data/prep/counties/clean_county_emb_16_17.csv")
df_20 <- read.csv("data/prep/counties/clean_county_emb_20.csv")
df_24 <- read.csv("data/prep/counties/clean_county_emb_24.csv")

dups <- function(df, name) {
  n_dup <- df %>% count(county_fips) %>% filter(n > 1) %>% nrow()
  cat(name, "- duplicate county_fips rows:", n_dup, "\n")
}

dups(df_16, "df_16")
dups(df_20, "df_20")
dups(df_24, "df_24")

train_join <- inner_join(
  df_16 %>% select(county_fips, rep_share_16 = rep_share),
  df_20 %>% select(county_fips, rep_share_20 = rep_share),
  by = "county_fips"
)

test_join <- inner_join(
  df_20 %>% select(county_fips, rep_share_20 = rep_share),
  df_24 %>% select(county_fips, rep_share_24 = rep_share),
  by = "county_fips"
)

cat("Rows train_join:", nrow(train_join), "\n")
cat("Unique county_fips train_join:", n_distinct(train_join$county_fips), "\n\n")

cat("Rows test_join:", nrow(test_join), "\n")
cat("Unique county_fips test_join:", n_distinct(test_join$county_fips), "\n")

```

```{r}

summary(df_16$rep_share)
summary(df_20$rep_share)
summary(df_24$rep_share)

range(df_16$rep_share, na.rm = TRUE)
range(df_20$rep_share, na.rm = TRUE)
range(df_24$rep_share, na.rm = TRUE)

```

```{r}
train_shift_vals <- train_join %>% mutate(SHIFT_16_20 = rep_share_20 - rep_share_16)
test_shift_vals  <- test_join  %>% mutate(SHIFT_20_24 = rep_share_24 - rep_share_20)

summary(train_shift_vals$SHIFT_16_20)
summary(test_shift_vals$SHIFT_20_24)

sd(train_shift_vals$SHIFT_16_20, na.rm=TRUE)
sd(test_shift_vals$SHIFT_20_24, na.rm=TRUE)


shift <- test_shift_vals$SHIFT_20_24
quantile(shift, c(.001,.01,.05,.5,.95,.99,.999), na.rm=TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# --- Load 2020 + 2024 with totalvotes kept ---
df_20 <- read_csv("data/prep/counties/clean_county_emb_20.csv") %>%
  select(county_fips, county_name, state_po,
         rep_share_20 = rep_share,
         totalvotes_20 = total_votes)

df_24 <- read_csv("data/prep/counties/clean_county_emb_24.csv") %>%
  select(county_fips,
         rep_share_24 = rep_share,
         totalvotes_24 = total_votes)

# --- Join + compute shift ---
shift_tbl <- inner_join(df_20, df_24, by = "county_fips") %>%
  mutate(
    SHIFT_20_24 = rep_share_24 - rep_share_20,
    votes_min   = pmin(totalvotes_20, totalvotes_24),
    votes_max   = pmax(totalvotes_20, totalvotes_24)
  )

# --- Bottom 25 (biggest negative shifts) ---
bottom25 <- shift_tbl %>%
  arrange(SHIFT_20_24) %>%
  slice(1:25) %>%
  select(county_fips, county_name, state_po,
         rep_share_20, rep_share_24, SHIFT_20_24,
         totalvotes_20, totalvotes_24, votes_min, votes_max)

# --- Top 25 (biggest positive shifts) ---
top25 <- shift_tbl %>%
  arrange(desc(SHIFT_20_24)) %>%
  slice(1:25) %>%
  select(county_fips, county_name, state_po,
         rep_share_20, rep_share_24, SHIFT_20_24,
         totalvotes_20, totalvotes_24, votes_min, votes_max)

cat("\nBottom 25 shifts (most negative):\n")
print(bottom25)

cat("\nTop 25 shifts (most positive):\n")
print(top25)

```

```{r}

library(dplyr)

var = 20207
og <- read.csv("data/prep/counties/countypres_2000-2024.csv")

# Your original check (still fine)
og[og$county_fips == var, c("county_fips", "party", "year", "candidatevotes", "totalvotes", "mode")]


shift_tbl[shift_tbl$county_fips == var, ]
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

og <- read.csv("data/prep/counties/countypres_2000-2024.csv")

pres_2024 <- og %>%
  filter(year == 2024) %>%
  mutate(
    office_up = toupper(trimws(as.character(office))),
    candidate = str_squish(as.character(candidate)),
    party     = str_squish(as.character(party)),
    cand_up   = toupper(candidate),
    party_up  = toupper(party)
  ) %>%
  filter(grepl("PRESIDENT", office_up))

# define the same "summary row" flag
pres_2024 <- pres_2024 %>%
  mutate(
    is_summary_row =
      grepl("TOTAL", cand_up) |
      grepl("VOTES CAST", cand_up) |
      ((party_up == "" | is.na(party_up)) &
         !is.na(candidatevotes) & !is.na(totalvotes) &
         candidatevotes == totalvotes)
  )

# For each county_fips: do we have ANY non-summary candidate rows?
county_summary_status <- pres_2024 %>%
  group_by(county_fips) %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_non_summary = sum(!is_summary_row, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# counties that would be fully dropped by summary-row removal
all_summary_counties <- county_summary_status %>%
  filter(n_non_summary == 0)

cat("2024 counties with ONLY summary rows:", nrow(all_summary_counties), "\n")
head(all_summary_counties, 20)
```
